evidence: Closure by verification against merged main (c81c06b2): every element of the filed resolution is stated on every named surface, audited element-by-element. Per-component boundary — the update op description now opens its partiality sentence with 'Because update applies per-component (a blocked schema rolls back only itself; the additive registry propagation writes regardless)…' (ops-registry.ts update op), and the same sentence ships in the package README (prose paragraph + op bullet) and the monorepo README (op bullet). Blocked rollback byte-exact — pre-existing on all three surfaces ('the schema and migrations.json stay byte-unchanged' in the op description; 'leaving BOTH the installed schema file AND the block file byte-unchanged' in both READMEs). Registry propagation + sibling schemas apply independently — stated by the shipped sentence ('…while applying registry additions or other-schema resyncs/migrations/merges'). CLI text surface — the reflecting CLI surfaces the op description, and the text-mode result renders the full result object (renderOpResultText, cli.ts:1147-1159) which carries the per-run partialApplication summary. SKILL.md regenerated from the built op strings. No implementation remains.

criteria_results.criterion: The context-update op description states the DEC-0018 per-component boundary
criteria_results.evidence: ops-registry.ts update op description: per-component sentence shipped; byte-unchanged rollback pre-existing in the same description
